subject: moose
one_liner: The human-in-the-loop data foundry that labels, ranks, and benchmarks every moose your models will ever meet.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan frontier-data infrastructure tone. Treats a single wild moose as a serious enterprise data category. Confident, technical, faintly bureaucratic. Numbers are stated flatly and never apologized for. Never winks at the camera. Reads like a Series C data foundry that has pointed its entire labeling apparatus at one ungulate and feels good about it.

hero.sub: Tine is the data foundry frontier labs trust to label, rank, and benchmark moose at scale. Eighteen hundred pounds of training signal, annotated down to the antler tine, with inter-annotator agreement we will put in the contract.

features.title: Pixel-perfect moose segmentation
features.blurb: A vetted workforce of field biologists draws bounding boxes, antler-tine keypoints, and pelage masks around an animal that is, on average, the size of a delivery van and visible from a moving train. Inter-annotator agreement holds above 98.4%.

features.title: RLHF, where the H is a moose
features.blurb: We collect pairwise preference data directly from the herd. Two forage options, one moose, one ranked judgment, logged. Your model learns to align with what a fourteen-hundred-pound ruminant actually prefers, which turns out to be willow.

features.title: MOOSE-bench evaluation suite
features.blurb: Twelve thousand held-out examples measuring exactly one capability: can your model tell a moose from a horse wearing a winter coat. Most frontier models score below 60%. We send you the number on a card.

pricing_tiers.name: Yearling
pricing_tiers.tagline: For teams labeling their first moose.
perks: Up to 240 annotated moose per month
Support answered within one rut cycle

pricing_tiers.name: Bull
pricing_tiers.tagline: Most teams land here. Their reasons vary.
perks: RLHF preference collection from the herd

pricing_tiers.name: Migratory
pricing_tiers.tagline: For organizations operating at herd scale.

testimonials.quote: Our model confidently labeled three elk as moose the week before launch. Tine caught all three. The fourth one was an actual moose, and they caught that too.
testimonials.name: Dr. Priya Venkataraman
testimonials.title: Head of Ungulate Ground Truth at a frontier lab she has asked us not to name

testimonials.quote: Our last vendor outsourced the labeling to people who had never met a moose. Tine's annotators have all met a moose. Several of them more than once.
testimonials.name: Gunnar Holt
testimonials.title: Director of Wildlife Data Operations, North Boreal Compute

testimonials.quote: I asked for inter-annotator agreement above 95 percent. They returned 98.4 and a photograph of the one moose that disagreed.
testimonials.name: Marisol Chen
testimonials.title: Staff ML Engineer, Antlerwise

demo_screen_concept: A specimen-annotation review station. A left rail holds the labeling queue: thumbnails of moose field photographs with per-item label status (pending, in review, accepted). The center stage shows the active specimen with a draggable bounding box, toggleable antler-tine keypoints, a pelage-mask overlay toggle, and a majesty-score slider from 1 to 10. A right rail shows the taxonomic label card in monospace: catalog number, species, confidence, inter-annotator agreement. Accept advances the queue and increments a throughput counter; reject sends it back with a flag.

design_direction.style: Museum specimen-archive rendered as a frontier-data brand. A bone specimen-card ground under a faint hairline registration grid, with framed natural-history plates carrying corner crop-marks, deckle-edge image frames, and taxonomic label blocks set in monospace. An oversized Fraunces masthead does the heavy lifting; imagery and type carry the page, not buttons. One rationed moss-green accent carries links, plate labels, and the single CTA. Hairline rules separate sections, generous institutional whitespace on an 8px rhythm, zero gradients, zero blur, zero drop-shadow cards. Every figure that pretends to be catalogue data (catalog numbers, agreement scores, throughput, prices) is set in tabular monospace. Reads like a natural-history institution's accession catalogue that has been handed, with total seriousness, to a frontier AI data-labeling team and pointed at a single moose.
design_direction.archetype: Museum-catalogue asymmetric editorial landing: a split masthead hero with an oversized wordmark and a framed specimen plate, an intro statement, three feature blocks separated by hairline rules, three pull-quote testimonials with specimen-card headshots, a three-tier accession-style pricing rack, and a demo handoff to the specimen-annotation review station.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Restrained and archival. Hairline link underlines that draw in on hover, 150-220ms opacity fades on section reveal, no parallax, no bounce. Motion is acknowledgement, never spectacle.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Asymmetric split. Oversized flush-left Fraunces 'Ground truth for moose.' on the left under a slim wordmark; on the right a framed natural-history plate of a single bull moose with corner crop-marks and a monospace taxonomic label block (catalog no., species, accession date). The CTA sits quietly under the intro statement, not hammered.

images.rationale: Museum-catalogue style is image-forward, so the hero earns a single framed specimen plate. The demo is photo-shaped (a specimen-annotation review queue), so two field photographs seat the active specimen and one queued item; the rest of the queue uses CSS specimen-card placeholders. Testimonials kept a CSS specimen-card monogram treatment rather than headshots, since the named users are humans and moose headshots would not read as their portraits. No testimonial images generated.

items.prompt: Editorial natural-history specimen photograph of a single adult bull moose with full broad palmate antlers, three-quarter profile, boreal-wetland backdrop, overcast diffuse light, bone-parchment/herbarium-ink/moss-green palette, fine grain, no text/logos/UI.
items.verdict: pass: correct bull moose with palmate antlers, muted naturalist palette on-direction, no text or UI artifacts

items.prompt: Naturalist field photograph of a single moose wading at the edge of a still boreal pond browsing aquatic willow, flat overcast daylight, muted bone/moss-green/ink palette, matte grain, no text/logos/UI.
items.verdict: pass: bull moose wading, documentary look, palette harmonizes, no artifacts

items.prompt: Naturalist field photograph of a single cow moose alert in a misty spruce clearing, full side profile with negative space, soft diffuse light, muted naturalist palette, matte grain, no text/logos/UI.
items.verdict: pass: cow moose in profile, atmospheric mist, on-palette, no artifacts
